Hi, I'm John Carlton. Over the last 30 years, I have earned a notorious reputation as one of the world's most respected advertising copywriters. I've written ads for the largest direct mail companies and ad agencies in existence, and created massively successful ad campaigns and launches for entrepreneurs and small businesses of all kinds. I have also written multiple bestselling books and courses to help people understand sales, marketing and copywriting. However, my biggest breakthrough is the simple writing system you are watching right now. See, I had an epiphany a few years ago. The exact process I have perfected over this 30-year career can be transformed into a simple step-by-step formula that can help anyone create their own ads and sales messages whenever you need a new ad or a new sales message. Over the years, the simple writing system has helped a massive mob of people to do exactly that. This group includes both rookie and professional copywriters who want to move up a level. And it includes new entrepreneurs and veteran business owners, especially those who never thought they'd be able to figure out how to create or write any kind of ad or sales message. But the simple writing system formula works. Some people who are terrified of writing now simply and quickly create their own stuff. Everything needed to make their career or business successful. And now you are one of that group. Okay, let's do a little introduction here so you get comfortable with what's about to happen to you. First of all, let's answer the question, why learn the pro-level secrets of writing copy? The answer is very simple, because nothing good will ever happen in your business or your career until the copy gets written. This is a fundamental principle of all business. Your ability to write something good, that is a sales message, is going to be the fuel for all of your business success. It's going to be in your ads, your emails, speeches, web copy, sales scripts, business planning and presentation, even down to video, social media, everything written, verbalized or filmed that you need to get your message out or to persuade prospects to become customers or to convince anyone to do anything. Now we have to answer a fundamental question that I get all the time when people talk about advertising. What is direct response advertising? Basically it's presenting your sales message and asking for a response to place an order to leave an email address, to get more information, to do something. That is the response we're looking for. It is not about slogans or vapid cleverness. Do not look to Madison Avenue for your successful advertising strategies. Basically, what you want to do is have your audience respond to your sales message in some manner. We'll talk about that a lot more, but you need to get straight on the difference between direct response advertising and what we call regular advertising. The stuff you see on television or you hear on the radio a lot that just doesn't really do anything. It just tries to create a brand awareness. Get away from that right now. Now do formulas work? I can tell you that 30 years of successful critically examined success says, yes, I've been using formulas my entire life. I started with simple ones like AIDA, attention, interest, desire, and action. That's the basic fundamental principle of a lot of direct response advertising. I later worked that into a system that became the formula which you are about to be presented with. It has been unconscious in my head for many, many years. It's just something that is automatic with me. Now that we've gotten it down and out on the film and on the paper, it's a very simple step-by-step process, a formula. Do this, then do this, then do this, then do this. Where is this ride taking you? Remember, this is the exact step-by-step process that I've used my entire career as an A-list, freelance, copywriter, and consultant. You are going to be able to use this to readjust your rational and emotional sense of what needs to be done simply, logically, and with total confidence so that when you sit down to write, you are prepared to craft the most persuasive and successful copy imaginable. So think about that for a minute. This takes away all of the fear of sitting down to write. This takes away all of the anxiety about what you're going to do to try to face down prospects who don't know who you are, or prospects you've never met if you're online, prospects that you're about to meet, who have no idea what the value is of you or your product or your services, and you're going to start to persuade them. You're going to know what to do step-by-step to get this done. Plus, a biggie for people who are afraid of writing, which includes a large part of the population, we're going to teach you how to murder writer's block because you will finally understand your project, your business, and your sales strategy better than ever the way top copywriters do with every job. This is seriously a professional level, complete course in writing persuasive, effective copy from a simple step-by-step formula. So you know what needs to be written and you know how to write it the right way, whether you do the actual writing or not. So if you need to write an email, you need to write a message or an ad, or even write a website or anything, you can do it. You will know how to do it. You know what needs to go into it. However, if you want to hire someone to do it for you, if you want to have somebody on your staff do it, then you know what goes into it so that you will not be fooled by someone who says, yeah, this is what you need. You will know. No, that's not what I need. I know what I need and I need you to follow the steps that I'm about to give you and you can help them write the ad that needs to be written. So you can do it yourself or if someone else does it for you, you will know what's going on. Take away all of the mysteries. So to get into this, please put aside your skepticism. Don't overthink this. Don't try to shortcut the steps. Don't assume anything. Just please surrender to the process. You can return to your skeptical, cynical self immediately after you've taken the simple writing system. But for now, let's get into it and I think you're off for a really fun ride. We put a lot of people through this program. They all enjoy it and they report life-changing transformational processes in their understanding of advertising, their understanding of business, their understanding of everything it takes to be successful and persuade people. Let's get going. I want to introduce my business partner, Stan Dahl, who I've known for 30 years. We met a long time ago in a Southern California beach town that we both happen to live in. Stan was involved in a different part of the business world. He was a consultant for some very, very large companies. And when the web started getting going back around the year 2002, 2003, I realized that things are really going to start happening and I was going to need some technical help and I also wanted his business acumen. So I asked him to come on board with me for the adventure that we were having when I was getting my business together, especially going online in a big way. So Stan and I had been business partners for a very, very long time. He was instrumental in putting the simple writing system together. I had the whole process was internalized for me for a very, very long time. And he started to realize that I was going through this kind of step-by-step process as I was breaking down ads when I was doing critiques, when I was creating ads on the fly, when we were doing hot seats. And so we started breaking it down into a step-by-step process. It took about a year to be able to get the simple writing system into its current format. We put way over a thousand people through this and found out that it's a lot better if we keep having Q&A sessions after this. So I want to have Stan kind of introduce why we're doing these little break sessions here in between the lessons. Thanks, John. I think that covered it, John. Yeah, the main thing is about the feedback. This is a hand holding process where we're trying to take you step by step. So I want to emphasize not to try to skip ahead. Just surrender to the process, to the formula. Do what we ask you to do. These are very simple exercises, yet they are profound in the way they cement everything in your head. I was one of the questions I was going to ask you, just kind of jump to. Commit to the process. One of the things I've seen as helping John administer this over the years is we've had a handful of people who fought the process. And some of those who I kind of, you might literally virtually had to kind of beat with a stick, but let's just do it in this order. We've thought this through. There's a reason. Have turned into some of our best success stories. So you may have some experience with copyrighting. You may have none. You may have your own process. You may have been in winging it, or this could all be new to you. Once you've done five ads, 10 ads, 20 ads, you, of course, you might tweak this. But we have, we've thought this through. Over a thousand people have gone through this successfully. There's a reason behind this. So just doing each exercise in order, commit to that. And you'll see it all comes together in the end. I realize now that might seem far away, but it's not. And just in a couple of weeks or a couple of hours, depending on how you're pacing yourself through the material, all of this gels into your first really good ad. And let me, let me just remind people that not only have rookie copyrighters gone through this and started careers that have now turned them into highly paid professional copywriters, already veteran professional copywriters have gone through this course to raise themselves up a level. So where they were middling low level copywriters, then they now have become A-list copywriters and copywriters that are commanding some of the highest fees out there. The same for businesses. There are veteran business owners who have done ad campaigns, who have done launches, who have done all of this. They just haven't understood what was going on. They hired writers to work for them. They never understood the process. It was all this big mystery to them. And this explains everything. This takes everything out of the realm of shrouded mystery and into a very real understandable against step-by-step formula. You will understand how ads that you had before worked, why they worked, and how to recreate that situation so your new ads work, whether you write them or someone else writes them. So it's not just for rookies and it's not just for writers. It is for anyone in business who wants to understand how the process of getting sales messages out into the world works. Yeah, I'm just going to add one group of people you didn't include, but we know we put a lot of people through the program. Our business owners who never see themselves sitting down to write an ad, but do not want to be 100% held hostage to or just trust that they're getting, I paid someone for an ad, I guess it's a good ad. If nothing else, if you go through this program, you're going to understand the process. You will be able to intelligently critique and talk to copywriters, even if you yourself don't think you're ever going to write a single word directly. In the end, it's your business, it's your responsibility. You want to have some idea if this body of words or this video script or this video you got back from somebody has any chance of selling. So now you're going to have a really good set of metrics and at least an intelligent conversation with a professional copywriter before you hire them to see if they really have any idea what they're doing. And all those thousands of people we've put through the program, there is no example of a human being who we haven't dealt with. We've dealt with the most stubborn people imaginable. Some, a lot of people were English was a second language. We have, this is an international program, although we do have to work in English, but we've had people from Asia, from Russia, all over Europe, Canada, foreign country. We've had people who have all kinds of strange ideas about how advertising should work, how the business world should work, how things should be happening, and part of this will be disabusing you of some of those notions. This is real world stuff. This is what has actually worked in the world. This is what will continue to work, and this is how you can be part of that, part of the business world that knows what to do, when to do it, how to do it, and how to make it work at the highest level possible. I think we've covered that. Part of the structure of the way we're presenting the material is to make it independent of any particular use. For example, you might see yourself as someone who's going to do a lot of video advertising, or someone who's going to do direct mail, or someone who's going to do text websites. None of that will matter as you're learning the material. Everything that we're going to cover, John's going to cover, is going to lend itself towards, you are now armed to go into what we'll just call a vehicle to deliver your message. If right now you're thinking about, well, I only need the stuff for video, or I only need the stuff for direct mail, or email, that's one way to go through it, but try to be a little open minded. Our goal here is whatever the next hot thing is, if suddenly there's a new social media outlet, or there's a new website, or there's a new whatever, you're going to know how to leap into that and successfully advertise through that mechanism. Because you're going to understand what motivates people, what influences people, and how to, as John said, get a response from them. How to get them, whether at that moment in time you're trying to get an email address, or close the sale, or sell just the click to your full website, that's what this course is designed to teach you. And you'll see very quickly, whether it's social media, or a billboard, or a TV, or a video. What's similar, or what is the same, overwhelms the specific differences. All right, there's just one question that I wanted to ask John at this point, because it's a very common one we get at people who are just starting the course. And it comes wrapped into the, all right John, like, hey, I've been watching commercials for years, I must know something about this. I see Coca-Cola does such and such and such, or hey, Apple just released a new product, and they did this, that. Shouldn't I do that? They run a lot of, they spend a lot of money on commercials and ads, they know what they're doing, they're successful. Why aren't you teaching me to do what Apple or Coca-Cola does? Yes, one of the reasons in the introduction I said don't look to Madison Avenue, which is the avenue where, if you watched the program Mad Men a long time ago, or you know that advertising agencies were centered on Madison Avenue in New York. So that's a shortcut word for basic advertising that is out there. Most of the advertising you see on television and in the print and even online is not well done, or it's done to promote what's called brand awareness, which is supposed to be a long-term game. Coca-Cola has had a century and billions of dollars put into their polar bears drinking Coca-Cola, the shape of the bottle. All of the things that you think are standard parts of advertising are really part of a unique idiosyncratic way of presenting a product. You don't have 100 years and you probably don't have several billion dollars to be able to get that kind of message out for your product. That's why direct response is so much better. It's a direct conversation with a prospect one at a time. It's more like your ad becomes your little salesman that goes out there, does your whole presentation, and even closes, even makes the sale happen. Whereas if you look at a lot of TV commercials that people see, say the Apple commercials, they don't have any kind of action they call for at all. They don't say come down to our store, they don't say call this number to get this, they don't say there's a special deal on things. But they have a different relationship with the culture than you do as an entrepreneur or as a copywriter or as a small business man. You can't expect most network ads to carry your ads, to run them during the Super Bowl, to get a lot of print space, to have all of the tech magazines do glowing articles and cover stories on you. You're not at that place. So to look at the people who are the companies that have massive amounts of resources and money and in time and in professionals working for them and all of the things that go in to the slick advertising you see on television, you aren't in a position to do that. Most small business men never will be. You don't need to be. You can make a very, very, very successful project without doing any kind of image advertising at all, doing very, very simple direct response advertising in different mediums. Some people get rich using just email. Some people get rich using just banner ads on other people's sites. Some people are just affiliate marketers. All of this is, there are multiple ways to be able to make your business successful. You aren't caught in one particular way. But to look at the big companies that have massive wads of cash to spend is not the way to decide how to do your business. You think that explains it? Yeah, I think so. I mean, another way I try to help people understand this is, let's use Coca-Cola as the first example. As soon as you are able, or we are able to reach into our device or screen and come back with a Coke, Coke will do the things we're going to talk about in the simple writing system. Yes. Right now, that's not the game Coke is playing. Coke is just trying to build a long-term brand awareness, make you feel happy, good thoughts about them. So when you are at the relatively few locations on the planet where, in that moment, you can acquire Coke, you pick them instead of another type of soda or a different, a cola or something else. So that is not the game we are going to try to teach you here. We are going to try to teach you how to get someone in that moment to respond, whether it's an opt-in or, of course, the big one is the buy now or the pick up the phone to call. So you are going to see some similarities, of course, but that's the fundamental thing you want to keep, we would like you to keep in mind. This is a very valuable skill and it's open to anybody. You don't need a huge budget. You don't need what Apple has. When you release a new product, the local news will not open with announcing your product to the world. There won't be a five-minute segment on national TV showing people queued up around the corner for your product. Apple is playing a different game than you. It's clearly a successful game. It's a good game, but we don't expect that to be your game right now. Okay. Okay. I think that lets, I'm sure people are kind of looking forward to diving in, so I think we'll wrap this up. If you're in the coaching program, this would be a good time. If you haven't done the introduction range, just to hop into the classroom, post-introduce yourself to your teacher, your fellow students, now would be a good time to do that. If you've done that already, then by all means, you want to jump right in to the next section and we'll start, John will start giving you some things you can put into action. Okay. Let's get going.
